raw_consumer_statement: I have been filing and refiling a dispute with AMEX, 8 times, since early XX/XX/2023. I have a letter from AMEX dated XX/XX/XXXX stating that " We are writing to inform you this as a result of our investigation, we've credited your account [ ... ] '' This was after I sent them a photograph of a technician 's ladder and tools in my room, next to a thermostat that had been removed from the wall, with the claim the resort hadn't delivered the product as described, which was a room with air conditioning. AMEX has subsequently, repeatedly, informed me that they were reversing their decision based on the merchant 's word that " no information of lack of air conditioning was received. '' Each time AMEX writes this to me, I call in and ask the agent to look at the files, and see that I have already provided photographic proof that the merchant was informed of the air conditioning problem, the agent agrees that my photograph proves they were informed, and they reopen the dispute. Then AMEX sends me back the exact same conclusion. This was a 5 star resort in a tropical climate, I had provided a record of the daily temperature, which was never below 82f, and 90f for a high, the air condition listed in the product description was an absolute essential that was not delivered until I was finally transferred to another room. In addition to my photo, I also have another agent of the merchant responding to my XXXX  complaint, acknowledging that they have record or my faulty air conditioner.
